reasoning:
The FRED series RESPPLLOPNWW represents the Fed's deferred asset (negative remittances due to operating losses). It has been steadily decreasing by an average of roughly $700M-$800M per week as the Fed accumulates losses, though there is occasional short-term positive variance (e.g., 2 of the last 14 weeks saw a net increase). 

The FRED tool provided a statistical combined probability anchor of 0.46. However, its seasonal component is upwardly biased because it compares last year’s smaller deferred asset level directly against this year’s much larger threshold limit, so I critically discounted this anchor for the short term, placing the May 18th estimate at 0.28 to blend the anchor with the strong empirical likelihood of continued weekly declines. 

Over longer horizons (August and November), the accumulated losses would form a deep hole, making a recovery mathematically very difficult under the current rate structure. However, longer timeframes introduce high structural uncertainty: a drastic macroeconomic shift requiring rapid Federal Reserve rate cuts to near-ZIRP levels could render the Fed hugely profitable, unwinding the accumulated losses by later in the year. Following explicit instructions, uncertainty must increase as the forecast horizon extends, so I have strictly scaled the predictions towards 0.5 to accurately account for long-tail structural break scenarios.
